title: Podman start in crontab get error ERRO[0158] Joining network namespace ... no such file or directory
body:
### Issue Description

Hello together, 
I try to start an podman container from linux crontab as *podmanuser*. The container failed hand and have impact on the other containers. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Run start podman container from crontab. 

```bash
$ crontab -l
6 9 * * * /usr/bin/podman start assay_info_create_images_prod
```

### Describe the results you received

Some seconds I get the error:
```
ERRO[0158] Joining network namespace for container ddac65285e6113fe37ae2998536372f43f593e4c310ed7e653b352c7e76d4979: error retrieving network namespace at /run/user/600/netns/netns-63bd4772-a5da-e6d4-7495-ae5cf8983717: failed to Statfs "/run/user/600/netns/netns-63bd4772-a5da-e6d4-7495-ae5cf8983717": no such file or directory
Error: error joining network namespace of container ddac65285e6113fe37ae2998536372f43f593e4c310ed7e653b352c7e76d4979: error retrieving network namespace at /run/user/600/netns/netns-63bd4772-a5da-e6d4-7495-ae5cf8983717: failed to Statfs "/run/user/600/netns/netns-63bd4772-a5da-e6d4-7495-ae5cf8983717": no such file or directory
```

All other containers have also an issue. The contaiers are in stopping state. 

### Describe the results you expected

no failed container and no impact on the other containers.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Is /tmp on a tmpfs? The runroot must be on tmpfs. Generally speaking every systemd distro should be using `/run/user/$UID/containers` as runroot instead, using the `/tmp/podman-run-600/containers` as fallback should be avoided at all costs.
This is likely the case because you did not login correctly, see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md#solution-28. You need to do a `podman system reset` to delete everything then podman can pick a new proper runroot for your system.

In any case this kind of error is fixed in the latest version as we no longer join the netns if not required so I am closing this.

Hello, the solution was the environment variable XDG_RUNTIME_DIR. I add this variable to crontab

```bash
XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=/run/user/600
11 2 * * * /usr/bin/podman start assay_info_create_images_prod;/usr/bin/podman wait assay_info_create_images_prod
```
and podman runs without error. 
Thanks for your help.
